race_name: L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 83, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 83 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France's paved mountain and valley roads through the forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 83 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,562ft of climbing on paved mountain and valley roads through the does to your power file on race day.
At L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 83 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved mountain and valley roads through the at tempo for 83 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 83 miles on paved mountain and valley roads through the. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France’s 83 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 83 miles of paved mountain and valley roads through the sierras de cordoba and punilla valley in Cordoba, Cordoba Province, Argentina (Estadio Mario Alberto Kempes start/finish).
